motivation: Let users add and remove note tags in the v2 UI and persist the full tag set through the existing note update API.

included: a compact Vue tag editor for the active note in edit mode
frontend tests that pin the existing UpdateNoteRequest.tags contract in services and useNotes
AppShell draft, save, cancel, and failure handling for note tag changes
full UI/backend regression verification after the frontend tag editing flow lands
excluded: backend production code, schemas, models, routes, migrations, or new tag endpoints; PUT /api/notes/{id} already supports full tag replacement
partial tag add/remove endpoints, a global tag-list endpoint, server-side tag filtering, tag colors, tag ordering, and bulk tag administration
new dependencies, router or Pinia adoption, or third-party icon libraries
design-token additions or hand edits to generated ui/src/design-system/tokens.css
unrelated documentation drift cleanup for previously-landed note create/edit/delete/tag persistence work

design_facts.stack: Mixed Vue 3/TypeScript/Vite frontend plus FastAPI/Python 3.13/SQLAlchemy async/PostgreSQL backend; DTCG design tokens; no router or Pinia.

token_manifest.system: W3C DTCG JSON built with Style Dictionary into CSS custom properties.
token_manifest.consumption_rule: New component CSS consumes semantic custom properties only; primitives are not used in new code.

work_items.title: Build compact note tag editor
work_items.design_reference: app shell edit pane tag editor

component_map.mapping: Custom presentational Vue SFC under ui/src/features/notes
component_map.notes: Typed props and emits; no HTTP calls; props down and update event up.
No new dependency and no new design-system abstraction unless existing DsToolButton is useful for the icon-sized controls.

behavior: renders current tags as stable-keyed chips with accessible remove buttons labelled with each tag
adds a tag from a text input on Enter or an add-button click

normalizes user input before emitting with backend-compatible rules: trim, remove one optional leading #, lowercase, collapse internal whitespace to hyphen, reject empty, reject values longer than 64 characters, and reject characters outside lowercase letters, numbers, hyphen, and underscore
deduplicates by normalized value without emitting a duplicate change
does not mutate props and does not call HTTP APIs
uses scoped styles and existing semantic CSS variables only

signals: contract-mutation:additive Vue component interface for NoteTagEditor props and update:tags emit
architectural-novelty:new user-facing frontend normalization path mirrors the backend tag contract

oracle.command: make test-ui-lint && cd ui && npm run test -- src/features/notes/NoteTagEditor.test.ts
assertions: NoteTagEditor emits update:tags with the normalized added tag when Enter or the add control is used.
NoteTagEditor emits update:tags without the removed tag when a chip remove button is used.
Blank input, invalid characters, over-64-character values, and duplicate normalized tags show a validation state without emitting a replacement list.
The component uses typed props/emits, stable v-for keys, scoped styles, PlusIcon, and XIcon.

consumes.service: ui/src/services/notes.ts updateNote(noteId, payload) and UpdateNoteRequest.tags?: string[] | null
consumes.composable: ui/src/features/notes/useNotes.ts updateNote(noteId, payload) maps the returned NoteDTO into the matching NoteMock

behavior: updateNote(noteId, { tags: [...] }) sends a PUT body with the tags array unchanged
updateNote(noteId, { tags: [] }) sends an empty array so the backend clears tags
content-only updates keep tags omitted from the PUT body
useNotes.updateNote replaces the matching local NoteMock.tags with backend-returned NoteDTO.tags, including the empty-array clear case

oracle.command: make test-ui-lint && cd ui && npm run test -- src/services/notes.test.ts src/features/notes/useNotes.test.ts
assertions: services/notes.test covers updateNote with a non-empty tags array and with tags: [].
services/notes.test keeps the existing content-only update payload assertion without tags.
useNotes.test covers updateNote mapping returned DTO tags back into the local notes ref, including clearing tags to [].

work_items.title: Wire tag editing into AppShell save flow

component_map.notes: Owns draftTags and persistence through useNotes.updateNote; no router or global store added.
Rendered in edit mode near the existing NoteEditor markdown form.
Remain filter-only; tag mutation happens in edit mode.

consumes.backend: existing PUT /api/notes/{note_id} full-replacement tag semantics: omitted/null preserves, [] clears, array replaces

state: startEdit seeds draftContent and draftTags from activeNote
cancelEdit, selectNote, openSettings, delete success, and create success reset draft tag state consistently with draftContent
save_behavior: tag edits are draft changes in edit mode; Save persists them and Cancel discards them
canSaveDraft is true when content changes or draftTags differs from activeNote.tags, while draftContent still satisfies the existing minimum length rule
saveDraft builds a PUT payload with content only when content changed and tags only when tags changed; an empty draftTags array is sent as tags: [] to clear all tags
successful saves use the returned NoteDTO through useNotes mapping, exit to read mode, and refresh visible tag chips/filter counts from notes
failed saves keep draftContent and draftTags visible and surface mutationError through the existing action-error
read-mode active-note tag buttons and SidebarDrawer TagFilter remain filter-only

signals: contract-mutation:existing AppShell save flow now conditionally sends UpdateNoteRequest.tags
blast-radius:touches the top-level shell state machine and its integration-style test file
capability-escalation:user-visible persisted tag writes through the existing notes update network path

oracle.command: make test-ui-lint && cd ui && npm run test -- src/features/shell/__tests__/AppShell.test.ts
assertions: AppShell edit mode displays the tag editor seeded from the active note tags.
Tag-only saves issue PUT /notes/{id} with tags and no content when content is unchanged.
Clearing all draft tags issues PUT /notes/{id} with tags: [].
Content-only saves preserve the existing content-only payload behavior when tags are unchanged.
Combined content and tag edits send both changed fields in one PUT payload.
Canceling edit mode or switching notes discards unsaved draft tag changes.
A failed tag save leaves the draft tags visible and shows the existing mutation error alert.
Read-mode tag filter interactions covered by existing tests still pass.

work_items.title: Run full tag editing regression gate

verifies: the merged binder result clears the repo full gate
backend tag persistence tests remain green without backend production changes
no new dependency is required for add/remove tag editing
contract.source_changes: No feature changes expected; only small test fixture or assertion corrections are allowed if the full gate exposes drift.

assertions: Full UI and backend gate passes after tag editing changes are merged.
Existing backend note tag persistence coverage remains green.
The final diff adds no third-party dependency for this feature.
